title: Looking Back on It
prose: Seven years is long enough that the early ones have started to feel like they happened to someone else — a first-year gawking at the ceiling of the Great Hall, a second-year worrying over exams that seem laughably small now, a person who hadn't yet met most of the people who matter most.
It's strange, catching sight of all that distance at once. Mostly, though, it feels like arriving somewhere, rather than just leaving somewhere behind.

choices.label: You're proud of how far you've come.
